figures.caption: Fig. 4: Trajectory visualization comparing our method with General Flow. The left column shows our results and the right column shows General Flow. The top row presents the indomain task close oven, while the bottom row shows pickup basket with an out-of-domain object.

Fig. 3: Partial visualization of motion affordances for representative tasks: open oven (left), pour water (middle), and cut fruit (right). The predicted 3D trajectories transfer effectively to real-robot execution across six affordance tasks.
TABLE II: Generalization results under object and scene changes across three representative tasks: pick, open, and cut. Best results in bold.

Fig. 5: Trajectory visualization of our model under CrossObject and Cross-Scene generalization settings. Figures (a) and (b) illustrate the model’s performance in cluttered scenes, while figures (c) and (d) show its behavior on unseen objects.
